steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a verb form question (Form, Structure, and Sense). The passage describes an ongoing, present-state scene: a lamp inside the Árbol de la sidra illuminates the glass as a permanent feature of the sculpture. The verb in the blank must match the simple present tense used throughout the passage (“is bathed,” “is”).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘will be illuminating’: Future progressive tense. The passage describes a habitual, current reality — not a future action. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘illuminates’: Simple present tense. Matches the tense of “is bathed” and “is” in the surrounding sentences, correctly describing the lamp’s permanent function. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘would illuminate’: Conditional form. The conditional implies uncertainty or hypothesis, but the lamp’s illumination of the glass is a stated fact, not a possibility. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘illuminated’: Simple past tense. The passage is in the present tense and describes an ongoing nightly phenomenon, not a past event. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
